premise: I poured my entire $210,000 401(k) into my daughter’s struggling hardware store for a 40% stake, and when a big-box competitor closed nearby, the business exploded to $1.2 million — so she and her husband forged a stock certificate showing I owned only 5% and forced me out with a $12,000 check. I proved the fraudulent conveyance, discovered they’d secretly stocked a second location, and in settlement I took possession of that fully-inventoried store and now undercut their prices by 15%, driving their flagship into Chapter 11.

thumb.detail: My Daughter Stole My 40% Stake And Forced Me Out With A $12K Check. So I Took He
